title: hieracium pratense subsp. flagellare
intro: Hieracium pratense subsp. flagellare, also known as hawkweed or false hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as skin irritations, wounds, and digestive problems, due to its ability to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and protect against infections. Herbal preparations of Hieracium pratense subsp. flagellare can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used externally to soothe skin irritations or internally to support digestive health.
